text.title: Principle Audit Rules
text.summary: Rules for running the six-principle audit as a binary gate, with antipatterns that cause false compliance.

rule.statement: "Passed: true" requires positive evidence quoting the artefact; absence of failure is not a pass.
rule.rationale: LLMs default to all-green outputs because they want to be helpful. Forced evidence prevents silent compliance inflation.

rule.statement: Any principle that fails must block the stage gate; the artefact must be fixed or escalated before the review concludes.
rule.rationale: Audit without a rejection consequence is theatre; the gate must actually stop work when a principle is violated.

rule.statement: Use exactly the six canonical names when auditing: Adopt Holistic View, Focus on Value, Embed Quality, Lead Accountably, Integrate Sustainability, Build Empowered Teams.
rule.rationale: Renamed principles produce inconsistent diffs and confuse cross-team governance reviews.

rule.statement: Integrate Sustainability requires a numeric proxy (CO2 estimate, accessibility WCAG level, social impact scope statement); a paragraph about sustainability values does not pass.
rule.rationale: Narrative sustainability is unmeasurable; numeric proxies create accountability and enable progress tracking.

rule.statement: Lock the PMBoK edition ("PMBoK 8, six principles") in every system prompt that runs a principle audit; never let the agent infer the edition.
rule.rationale: LLMs conflate PMBoK 7 twelve principles with PMBoK 8 six principles; edition ambiguity produces incorrect audits.

antipattern.description: All six principles pass without any explicit evidence quotes from the artefact.
antipattern.reason: All-pass outputs without quotes are LLM politeness artefacts; require citations for every passed principle.

antipattern.description: Invoking "Build Empowered Teams" to justify removing governance or oversight.
antipattern.reason: Empowerment requires working agreements and decision rights, not absence of process; require an offsetting governance artefact.

antipattern.description: Using "Quality" as a standalone domain label (PMBoK 6 vocabulary) inside a PMBoK 8 audit.
antipattern.reason: PMBoK 8 does not have a Quality domain; quality is the Embed Quality principle applied across all domains.
